meta.notes: Editable — add your own items following docs/question-format.md

questions.prompt: A 2 kg object accelerates at 3 m/s². What net force acts on it?
questions.explanation: Newton's second law: F = ma = 2 kg × 3 m/s² = 6 N.
questions.hint: Use F = ma.

questions.prompt: Which quantity is a vector?
questions.explanation: Velocity has both magnitude and direction. Speed is the scalar magnitude of velocity.
questions.hint: Vectors have direction.

questions.prompt: How much work is done when a 10 N force moves an object 4 m in the direction of the force?
questions.explanation: Work W = Fd = 10 N × 4 m = 40 J when force and displacement are parallel.
questions.hint: Multiply force by distance.

questions.prompt: When a gas in a sealed container is heated at constant volume, what primarily increases?
questions.explanation: At constant volume, added heat raises molecular kinetic energy, increasing temperature and pressure.
questions.hint: Temperature relates to average molecular KE.

questions.prompt: A wave has frequency 500 Hz and wavelength 0.68 m. What is its speed?
questions.explanation: v = fλ = 500 Hz × 0.68 m = 340 m/s (approximately the speed of sound in air).
questions.hint: Use v = f × λ.

questions.prompt: A converging lens forms a real inverted image when the object is placed:

choices.text: Between the lens and focal point
At twice the focal length only

questions.explanation: For a converging lens, objects beyond F produce real, inverted images on the opposite side.
questions.hint: Real images require the object outside the focal point.

questions.prompt: A 3 kg mass is lifted 4 meters at constant speed. What is the work done against gravity? (Use g=9.8 m/s²)
questions.hint: Use W = mgh.

questions.prompt: Which quantity is a scalar quantity?
questions.explanation: Speed is a scalar because it has magnitude only, while displacement, velocity, and acceleration are vectors.
questions.hint: Scalars do not include direction.

questions.prompt: A 12 V battery is connected across a 3 Ω resistor. What electric current flows through the resistor?
questions.explanation: Using Ohm's law I = V/R = 12 V ÷ 3 Ω = 4 amperes.
questions.hint: Apply I = V/R.

questions.prompt: Which form of energy is stored when a spring is compressed?
questions.explanation: A compressed spring stores elastic potential energy.
questions.hint: Energy stored due to position or configuration is potential energy.

questions.prompt: Which statement best distinguishes speed from velocity?

choices.text: Speed includes direction, velocity does not.
Velocity includes direction, speed does not.
Speed is a vector, velocity is a scalar.
Speed and velocity are always equal in magnitude.

questions.explanation: Velocity is a vector quantity with direction; speed is a scalar and has no direction.
questions.hint: One of these terms requires direction.

questions.prompt: A 10 ohm resistor has 2 amperes of current. What voltage is across it?
questions.explanation: Using V = IR, voltage = 10 Ω × 2 A = 20 V.
questions.hint: Apply Ohm's law.

questions.prompt: Which material allows electrons to move freely and conducts electricity well?
questions.explanation: Conductors such as metals allow electrons to move freely and thus conduct electricity well.
questions.hint: This type of material is used for wires.

questions.prompt: A lens has a focal length of 20 cm. Where will an object placed 40 cm from the lens form an image?
questions.explanation: Using the lens formula 1/f = 1/do + 1/di gives 1/20 = 1/40 + 1/di, so di = 40 cm.
questions.hint: Solve the thin lens equation for image distance.

questions.prompt: Which type of wave requires a medium to travel?
questions.explanation: Sound waves are mechanical and require a medium to propagate, unlike electromagnetic waves.
questions.hint: Consider which waves can travel through a vacuum.

questions.prompt: A car travels 180 km in 3 hours. What is its average speed?
questions.explanation: Average speed is distance divided by time: 180 km ÷ 3 h = 60 km/h.
questions.hint: Use total distance over total time.

questions.prompt: Which phenomenon causes light to bend when it enters a glass prism?
questions.explanation: Refraction is the bending of light as it passes between media with different refractive indices.
questions.hint: This effect occurs because light changes speed in different materials.

questions.prompt: A 60 W light bulb operates for 2 hours. How much energy does it use?
questions.explanation: Energy = power × time = 60 W × 2 h = 120 Wh; however since 2 hours times 60 W is 120 Wh, wait the answer should be 120 Wh. I'll set correctChoiceId to C.
questions.hint: Multiply the wattage by operating time in hours.

questions.prompt: Which physical quantity is conserved in an isolated system when objects collide?
questions.explanation: Momentum is conserved in collisions within isolated systems; kinetic energy may or may not be conserved.
questions.hint: This quantity depends on both mass and velocity.

questions.prompt: A train covers 150 km in 2.5 hours. What is its average speed?
questions.explanation: Average speed = distance ÷ time = 150 km ÷ 2.5 h = 60 km/h. Wait, 150 / 2.5 = 60, so correctChoiceId should be C.
questions.hint: Divide the total distance by total travel time.

questions.prompt: Which law states that the force on a charged particle is the product of charge, velocity, and magnetic field?
questions.explanation: The Lorentz force law describes the magnetic force on a moving charge: F = q(v × B).
questions.hint: This law involves magnetic fields and moving charges.

questions.prompt: A 5 kg object moves at 4 m/s. What is its kinetic energy?
questions.explanation: Kinetic energy = ½mv² = 0.5 × 5 × 16 = 40 J. Wait correction: 0.5 × 5 × 16 = 40 J, so correctChoiceId should be B.
questions.hint: Use KE = ½mv² with velocity squared.

questions.prompt: Which quantity is measured in newtons?
questions.explanation: Force is measured in newtons, where 1 N = 1 kg·m/s².
questions.hint: Think of F = ma units.

questions.prompt: A 1 kg mass is dropped from rest and falls 5 m. What is its potential energy at the start using g=9.8 m/s²?
questions.explanation: Potential energy = mgh = 1 × 9.8 × 5 = 49 J.
questions.hint: Use the formula mgh for gravitational potential energy.

questions.prompt: Which type of energy is stored in chemical bonds?
questions.explanation: Chemical potential energy is stored in the arrangement of atoms and bonds within molecules.
questions.hint: This energy is released during chemical reactions.

questions.prompt: A spring with spring constant 200 N/m is compressed 0.1 m. What is its stored elastic potential energy?
questions.explanation: Elastic potential energy = ½ k x² = 0.5 × 200 × 0.01 = 1.0 J.
questions.hint: Square the displacement and multiply by half the spring constant.

questions.prompt: Which quantity is conserved in an elastic collision?
questions.explanation: In elastic collisions, both momentum and kinetic energy are conserved.
questions.hint: This type of collision does not lose energy to deformation or heat.

questions.prompt: A wave has a frequency of 250 Hz and a period of:
questions.explanation: Period = 1 / frequency = 1 / 250 Hz = 0.004 s, so correctChoiceId should actually be B.
questions.hint: The period is the reciprocal of frequency.

questions.prompt: Which term describes the spread of a wave as it passes through a narrow opening?
questions.explanation: Diffraction is the bending and spreading of waves when they encounter an obstacle or aperture.
questions.hint: This effect is strongest when the opening is similar in size to the wavelength.

questions.prompt: A 50 N force moves an object 3 m in the same direction. How much work is done?
questions.explanation: Work = force × distance = 50 N × 3 m = 150 J.
questions.hint: Multiply force by displacement in the same direction.
